item.title: Delivery Surge: 237 Stocks Show Institutional Activity This Week
item.description: This week’s market activity was marked by significant institutional participation, as evidenced by a surge in delivery volumes and traded values across a broad range of stocks. Despite the absence of extreme delivery percentage signals indicating exclusive buying or selling, the high volume and value confirmations suggest a balanced but active engagement by large investors. Time: 21 minutes ago

item.title: Breakout Alert: 59 Technical Signals Reveal Market Bias and Key Patterns This Week
item.description: This week’s technical pattern activity highlights a bullish tilt across the market, with a notable surge in moving average crossovers and price gaps signalling potential momentum shifts. Sectoral and market capitalisation trends reveal contrasting sentiments, offering investors a nuanced view of the evolving landscape. Time: 21 minutes ago

item.title: Volume Explosion: 15 Stocks Saw Exceptional Trading Activity This Week
item.description: This week witnessed a pronounced surge in trading volumes across multiple sectors, with a notable concentration in banking and technology stocks. The market experienced a high number of circuit breaker events, reflecting heightened volatility and active investor participation. Time: 22 minutes ago

item.title: Rating Revisions Surge: 362 Upgrades and 239 Downgrades This Week
item.description: This week witnessed a notable surge in stock score adjustments across multiple sectors, with a total of 601 grade changes and 1337 dot rating updates recorded. The predominance of upgrades over downgrades, particularly in the Non Banking Financial Company (NBFC) sector, signals evolving market dynamics and shifting investor evaluations. Time: 22 minutes ago

item.title: 186 Stocks Hit 52-Week Highs This Week: Pharma and Heavy Electrical Lead
item.description: This weekly saw a pronounced surge in price strength across select sectors, with Pharmaceuticals & Biotechnology, Heavy Electrical Equipment, and Garments & Apparels leading the charge in 52-week highs. Meanwhile, certain sectors such as Computers - Software & Consulting and NBFCs faced notable pressure, reflecting a mixed but predominantly large-cap driven market environment. Time: 22 minutes ago

item.title: KSE Ltd is Rated Sell by MarketsMOJO
item.description: KSE Ltd is rated 'Sell' by MarketsMOJO, with this rating last updated on 16 February 2026.
